steps.title: Identify the contrast needing correction
steps.content: The sentence structure is “Chaucer didn't write in Modern English; [blank] he wrote in Middle English.” The key signal is that the second clause provides an alternative to the negation in the first clause—this is a direct correction.

steps.title: Match the correction to the choices
steps.content: ”Rather” introduces a correction or alternative using the pattern “not X; rather, Y.” The other options introduce causation, comparison, and sequence, which don't fit the corrective logic here. That matches choice A — A is correct.
